options.option: I have problem keeping backhands at play.
I can hit them in but avoid them when possible.
I have moderate success with my backhand.
I'm effective with pace and placement.
I have mastered pace, placement, touch and spin.

fields.question: How would you describe your serve?

options.option: I have trouble getting my serve in.
I consistently get my serve in but can't control depth.
I have control over the depth and speed of my serve.
I serve with power and sometimes vary speed and spin.
I've mastered power, accuracy, septh, speed and spin.

fields.question: How would you describe your dinking abilities?

options.option: I rarely dink or have trouble sustaining a dink rally.
I'm sometimes able to sustain a dink rally.
I have good control but sometimes lose patience.
I can vary shot types and know which balls to attack.
I strategically use pace and placement to create opportunities.

fields.question: How would you describe your third shot?

options.option: I just try to get it over.
I'm still working on a drop shot to get to the net.
I mix drop shots with power shots, but am inconsistent.
I consistently execute a 3rd shot that can't easily be attacked.
I've mastered the 3rd shot on both the forehand and  backhand.

fields.question: How would you describe your volley?

options.option: I have trouble controlling my direction.
I'm able to control a medium paced volley.
I'm able  to volley shots at a variety of speeds.
I can block hard shots into the NVZ and putaway overheads.
I've mastered blocks, swinging volleys, overheads and putaways.

fields.question: How would you describe your strategy?

options.option: I understand basic positioning and scoring.
I consistently get to the net and have basic knowledge of stacking.
I understand stacking & look for opponent's weaknesses.
I move in all directions, easily stack and adjust strategy mid-game.
I've mastered various strategies and adjust in competitive matches.

fields.name: Pickleit Ball
fields.description: Regulation pickleball balls weigh between 0.78oz (22.11g) to 0.935oz (26.51g). Heavier balls tend to be used on outdoor courts, and lighter balls tend to be used on indoor courts, but there is no official distinction between indoor and outdoor balls. As long as a pickleball ball weighs between 0.78oz (22.11g) to 0.935oz (26.51g), it can be used on any indoor or outdoor pickleball court.
fields.specifications:
Be a single, consistent colour
Have between 26 and 40 holes
Be between 40 and 50 on the Durometer D scale (a measure of hardness)
Have a bounce of 30-34 inches when dropped from 78 inches

fields.name: Pickleball Paddle 500 - Blue
fields.description:
We created the 500 paddle for intermediate Pickleball players who practice very regularly.

BENEFITS
handling: Easy to handle thanks to its weight (225g) and 23.2 cm balance.
vibration dampening: Thin honeycombing and 100% graphite faces cushion impacts.
forgiveness: Forgiving to centering errors thanks to its wide, thick head.
ergonomic grip: Comfortable in hand thanks to its soft, textured grip.
precision: Stiff graphite head for a sharper, more precise feel
fields.specifications:
Level of practice	Intermediate
Number of rackets	1 racket
Racket type	Versatile
Condition	New
Weight (in g)	225 g
Main material	Graphite
Sold in set	Sold alone

fields.name: 750ml Soft Touch Insulated Water Bottle - White - EritVille Gift Company
fields.description: If you are looking for a water bottle that can keep your drinks at the right temperature for hours, you might want to check out this product. It is a double walled vacuum insulated stainless steel water bottle that has a soft touch coating for a comfortable grip. The lid is leakproof, so you don’t have to worry about spills or leaks. The water bottle can keep your drinks hot for 5 hours or cold for 15 hours, depending on your preference. The capacity is 750ml, which is enough for your daily hydration needs. This water bottle is a great choice for anyone who wants a durable, stylish and functional water bottle.
